title: Community Mental Health Agencies * African American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Community Mental Health Agencies * African American Community 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OHSU AVEL GORDLY CENTER FOR HEALING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally responsive mental health services, including African American–focused services. Offers individual, group, couples, and family counseling, as well as medication support. Helps adults, couples, and families address concerns such as depression, anxiety, trauma, major life changes, substance use, DUI treatment, problem gambling, and ongoing health conditions. Works to reduce stigma around mental health and encourage healing. 621 SW Alder Street Suite 520 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Sliding scale fees for mental health services are available to uninsured Multnomah County residents who identify as African or African American and have incomes at or below 200% of the federal poverty level. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-6pm, Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 418-5311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU AVEL GORDLY CENTER FOR HEALING
